Calls to `_kill_process_group` in src/kiss/agents/sorcar/useful_tools.py appear in three distinct enclosing functions/methods (plus its own definition at line 418):

1. **`_stop_monitor`** (module-level function, line 447) — calls it at line 459 when the stop event fires.
2. **`UsefulTools.Bash`** (method, line 856) — calls it at lines 913 (on `subprocess.TimeoutExpired`) and 932 (in the `BaseException` handler).
3. **`UsefulTools._bash_streaming`** (method, line 984) — calls it at lines 1038 (deadline hit without EOF), 1056 (process didn't exit within 5s after EOF), and 1060 (in the `BaseException` handler).

Note: line 1056's call is lexically inside `_bash_streaming` itself (not in the nested `_drain_stdout` helper). So the distinct enclosing function/method names are: `_stop_monitor`, `Bash`, and `_bash_streaming`.

The module-level helper `_format_bash_result` (defined at line 409 of src/kiss/agents/sorcar/useful_tools.py) is called from two distinct enclosing methods:

The private helper `_catalog_xml` (defined at line 318 of src/kiss/agents/sorcar/skills.py) is used by the function **`make_skill_tool`** (defined at line 401). Specifically, inside `make_skill_tool`, the nested `skill` tool function's docstring is built with an f-string that calls `_catalog_xml(skills)` (line 448) to embed the XML catalog of available skills into the tool's documentation.

The private helper `_catalog_xml` is used by the function `make_skill_tool(work_dir)` in src/kiss/agents/sorcar/skills.py. Inside `make_skill_tool`, the result of `_catalog_xml(skills)` is embedded into the dynamically assigned docstring of the nested `skill` tool function (`skill.__doc__ = (... f"{_catalog_xml(skills)}\n\n" ...)`), so the agent sees the token-efficient `<available_skills>` catalog in the tool's description.

The module-level function `_bash_parent_repo_guard` (defined at line 178 of src/kiss/agents/sorcar/useful_tools.py) is called by the `Bash` method of the `UsefulTools` class. Specifically, at line 876, `UsefulTools.Bash` calls `guard = _bash_parent_repo_guard(command, self.work_dir)` near the start of the method, and if the guard returns a non-None message, `Bash` returns that guard message instead of executing the command. It is the only caller in the file.

The module-level function `_bash_parent_repo_guard` (defined at line 178 of src/kiss/agents/sorcar/useful_tools.py) is called exactly once, at line 876, inside the `Bash` method of the `UsefulTools` class (method defined at line 856, class at line 463). The call is:

```python
guard = _bash_parent_repo_guard(command, self.work_dir)
```

So the caller is `UsefulTools.Bash`.

According to the `Skill` dataclass docstring in `src/kiss/agents/sorcar/skills.py`, the `source` attribute indicates where the skill was found, and its possible values are:

1. `"bundled"`
2. `"claude-user"`
3. `"agents-user"`
4. `"user"`
5. `"claude-project"`
6. `"agents-project"`
7. `"project"`

`bundled_skills_dir()` in `src/kiss/agents/sorcar/skills.py` returns `Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "claude_skills"` — i.e., the `agents/claude_skills` directory relative to the kiss package (`kiss/agents/claude_skills`, located at `src/kiss/agents/claude_skills` in the repo). Since `skills.py` lives in `kiss/agents/sorcar/`, going up two parents lands in `kiss/agents/`, then appends `claude_skills`.

The script that downloads content into it is `scripts/fetch-claude-skills.sh`. That script resolves the project root, creates `$PROJECT_ROOT/src/kiss/agents/claude_skills` if missing, and (idempotently) sparse-clones `github.com/anthropics/claude-code`, copying each `plugins/*` directory into `claude_skills/`. Each downloaded plugin holds skills at `<plugin>/skills/<name>/SKILL.md`, which are namespaced as `<plugin>:<name>`.

`bundled_skills_dir()` in `src/kiss/agents/sorcar/skills.py` returns the `agents/claude_skills` directory relative to the kiss package — i.e., `Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "claude_skills"`, which resolves to `src/kiss/agents/claude_skills`. Content is downloaded into it by the script `scripts/fetch-claude-skills.sh`, which fetches the official Claude Code plugins (each plugin holding skills at `<plugin>/skills/<name>/SKILL.md`). The directory may be absent in development checkouts.

The `KISSAgent.run` method in src/kiss/core/kiss_agent.py has the following parameters (besides `self`):

1. `model_name: str` — the name of the model to use for the agent
2. `prompt_template: str` — the prompt template for the agent
3. `arguments: dict[str, str] | None = None` — arguments substituted into the prompt template
4. `system_prompt: str = ""` — optional system prompt for the model
5. `tools: list[Callable[..., Any]] | None = None` — tools available to the agent
6. `is_agentic: bool = True` — whether the agent is agentic
7. `max_steps: int | None = None` — maximum number of steps
8. `max_budget: float | None = None` — maximum budget
9. `model_config: dict[str, Any] | None = None` — model configuration
10. `printer: Printer | None = None`
11. `verbose: bool | None = None`
12. `attachments: list[Attachment] | None = None`
13. `print_prompts: bool = True`

Any five of these (e.g., model_name, prompt_template, arguments, system_prompt, tools) answer the question.
